purpose: Production-readiness backlog for the Fastify Shopify integration backend.

tasks.title: Stop exposing Shopify GIDs as the primary public API model
tasks.rationale: The current product, variant, cart, and cart-line DTOs expose Shopify global IDs directly. This couples the mobile app to Shopify internals and makes provider migration, replay protection, and authorization boundaries harder.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce app-level productId, variantId, cartId, and cartLineId aliases or opaque IDs.
Keep Shopify GIDs behind server-side mapping/encoding logic.
Mobile app no longer needs to understand gid://shopify/... identifiers except through opaque backend contracts.
Backward compatibility plan exists if current clients already consume GIDs.

tasks.title: Normalize Shopify userErrors into stable domain error codes
tasks.rationale: The backend currently returns Shopify userErrors fields, messages, and codes inside details. That leaks provider semantics and creates unstable mobile behavior if Shopify changes wording or field paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Map Shopify userErrors to backend-owned codes such as CART_LINE_UNAVAILABLE, CART_EXPIRED, INVALID_VARIANT, QUANTITY_UNAVAILABLE.
Return user-safe messages that do not expose Shopify field paths.
Log raw Shopify error metadata internally with request correlation.
Add tests for each mapped cart failure mode.

tasks.title: Separate Storefront pagination cursors from public pagination tokens
tasks.rationale: The product list response returns Storefront cursors directly. Cursors are upstream implementation details and can be tampered with or become invalid across provider changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose opaque nextPageToken/previousPageToken values.
Validate and decode tokens server-side.
Reject malformed tokens with a stable API error.
Document token expiry and compatibility rules.

tasks.title: Add authentication and cart ownership model
tasks.rationale: Anyone with a cart ID can fetch or mutate that cart. Shopify cart IDs are bearer-like identifiers; without app auth and ownership binding, cart tampering and checkout URL disclosure are possible.
acceptanceCriteria: Define auth strategy for mobile clients.
Bind carts to authenticated user/session/device identity.
Reject cart operations when ownership does not match.
Add tests for cross-user cart access attempts.

tasks.title: Add API rate limiting and abuse protection
tasks.rationale: Catalog search and cart mutations proxy directly to Shopify. Without per-IP/user/device limits, the backend can amplify abuse into upstream rate limits and cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Fastify rate limit plugin with route-specific budgets.
Use stricter budgets for cart mutations and checkout URL creation.
Use distributed storage such as Redis for multi-instance deployments.
Return Retry-After headers and stable RATE_LIMITED error responses.
Add metrics for limited requests.

tasks.title: Lock down CORS and production docs exposure
tasks.rationale: CORS defaults to wildcard and Swagger UI is always registered. That is fine for a POC, but production should restrict origins and avoid public interactive docs unless intentionally protected.
acceptanceCriteria: Fail startup in production when CORS_ORIGIN is wildcard.
Support allowlisted mobile/web origins.
Gate Swagger UI behind NODE_ENV or auth.
Document production CORS and docs policy.

tasks.title: Harden request validation for Shopify identifiers and body sizes
tasks.rationale: Current validation only checks non-empty strings for cart IDs, line IDs, and merchandise IDs. This permits oversized or malformed payloads to reach downstream logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add maximum string lengths for all IDs.
Validate expected opaque ID format or encoded token format.
Configure Fastify bodyLimit.
Reject unknown body fields where appropriate.
Add fuzz/negative validation tests.

tasks.title: Add upstream timeouts, aborts, and retry policy for Shopify calls
tasks.rationale: Storefront fetch calls currently have no explicit timeout. Slow upstream responses can consume request slots and create cascading latency under load.
acceptanceCriteria: Use AbortController with a configurable Shopify timeout.
Retry only safe/idempotent reads with bounded exponential backoff.
Do not blindly retry cart mutations unless idempotency is designed.
Return stable timeout errors and log upstream latency.

tasks.title: Add circuit breaker and Shopify degradation strategy
tasks.rationale: A Shopify outage currently means every request attempts another upstream call. Under incident conditions this worsens latency and thread/socket pressure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a circuit breaker around Storefront API calls.
Serve cached catalog reads when breaker is open.
Fail cart mutations fast with actionable errors.
Expose breaker state in operational metrics.

tasks.title: Introduce product catalog caching
tasks.rationale: Every product list/search/detail call hits Shopify. Product catalog reads are high-volume and mostly cacheable, so this is the first obvious bottleneck.
acceptanceCriteria: Cache product list and detail responses with short TTLs.
Use separate keys for query, first, and pagination token.
Add stale-while-revalidate behavior if feasible.
Invalidate or refresh via webhook/admin sync later.
Measure cache hit ratio.

tasks.title: Reduce product query payload size and make image/variant limits configurable
tasks.rationale: The backend fetches 10 images and 100 variants per product for every list request. This can become expensive and slow for large catalogs.
acceptanceCriteria: Use lighter list DTOs and reserve full variant/image payloads for detail pages where possible.
Make image and variant limits configurable.
Add response size metrics.
Load-test catalog list latency with realistic products.

tasks.title: Design cart mutation serialization or conflict handling
tasks.rationale: Multiple concurrent add/update/remove requests for the same cart can race. The last Shopify response wins and the client can reconcile stale state.
acceptanceCriteria: Define per-cart mutation ordering strategy.
Use queueing, version checks, or server-side idempotency keys.
Return conflict responses where mutation order cannot be guaranteed.
Add concurrent mutation tests.

tasks.title: Add idempotency support for create cart and add lines
tasks.rationale: Mobile retries, flaky networks, or repeated taps can create duplicate carts or duplicate lines.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept Idempotency-Key headers for cart creation and line adds.
Persist idempotency results with TTL.
Return previous result for duplicate idempotency keys.
Document retry semantics for the mobile app.

tasks.title: Add request correlation, metrics, and tracing
tasks.rationale: Structured logging exists, but there are no request IDs, upstream latency metrics, Shopify operation tags, cart mutation counters, or distributed traces.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate or accept request IDs.
Emit structured logs with route, operation, status, duration, and request ID.
Add Prometheus/OpenTelemetry metrics.
Trace Shopify calls separately from Fastify route handling.
Redact tokens and sensitive IDs consistently.

tasks.title: Upgrade health checks to readiness and liveness checks
tasks.rationale: The current health endpoint only returns ok and does not prove env/config readiness, Shopify reachability, cache readiness, or DB/Redis readiness once added.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose /health/live and /health/ready.
Readiness checks required dependencies without creating heavy load.
Return machine-readable dependency status.
Integrate with deployment probes.

tasks.title: Replace demo orders with a production order history strategy
tasks.rationale: Demo orders are hard-coded and contain fake merchandise IDs. This must not ship as production order history.
acceptanceCriteria: Choose customer-token Storefront order fetching, webhook-backed order projection, or Admin API mediated backend service.
Remove hard-coded demo orders from production builds.
Model order ownership and authentication.
Ensure purchase-again uses real current variant IDs or mapping rules.

tasks.title: Implement checkout completion reconciliation
tasks.rationale: Returning checkoutUrl alone is fine for opening checkout, but production needs backend confirmation of payment/order state rather than trusting a client-side URL transition.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Shopify webhook handling for checkout/order completion.
Verify webhook HMAC signatures.
Persist order state and expose app-friendly order detail endpoint.
Handle duplicate webhooks idempotently.
Do not trust mobile completion signals as payment proof.

tasks.title: Add persistent storage for users, carts, idempotency, and orders
tasks.rationale: The backend is currently stateless. Production features like cart ownership, idempotency, order projection, and audit trails require durable storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Select database and migration tool.
Create schemas for users/sessions, carts, cart ownership, idempotency keys, orders, webhook events.
Add repository abstractions and tests.
Define retention and privacy policies.

tasks.title: Expand integration, contract, and load tests
tasks.rationale: Current tests cover happy-ish service/routes and repository mapping. Production needs contract tests, concurrency tests, upstream failure tests, and performance tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add API contract tests for all routes and error envelopes.
Add Storefront client tests for non-JSON, timeout, 429, and GraphQL error responses.
Add concurrent cart mutation tests.
Add load tests for product search and cart mutation paths.
Add CI gates for lint/typecheck/test/build.

tasks.title: Productionize deployment, secrets, and runtime configuration
tasks.rationale: The project has local env validation but no deployment artifact, secret manager integration, or runtime hardening.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Dockerfile or deployment-specific build artifact.
Load secrets from a secret manager in production.
Set NODE_ENV=production and validate required production-only settings.
Define horizontal scaling model and graceful shutdown.
Add security headers where applicable.
